one_time_description: Large deferred revenue balance of $14,641,043 recognized in year including $5,000,000 OSR replacement COVID-19 funding and $4,459,300 FNWWAP water project, significantly inflating revenue
economic_notes: Woodstock First Nation Economic Development Corporation includes Eagle's Nest and three retail fuel operations plus cannabis retail (WFN Cannabis). Partnership in Wisokolamson Energy LP wind power facility in Albert County NB receiving $100,000 or 2.4% of income annually. Province of NB gave 12 months notice to terminate tobacco tax, gasoline/motive fuel tax and HST collection agreement expiring January 2023, expected to have significant financial impact.
notes: Fiscal year end March 31, 2022. Province is New Brunswick. Deferred revenue movement of $14.4M dominates cash flows and inflates reported revenue significantly — underlying operations are more modest. The Economic Development Corporation (GBE) is accounted for on modified equity basis and includes Eagle's Nest, three fuel bars, and cannabis retail with $50.9M in economic activity revenue and $42.6M in corresponding expenses. Prior year auditor was different firm. Province of NB terminating tax collection agreement in January 2023 — material risk to future revenues. Remuneration schedule referenced on page 34 but data not included in extracted text. Net debt position of $2,154,574. Expenses total includes amortization of $2,431,176 reported separately from program expenses. Prior year expenses calculated as $42,597,312 program expenses plus $2,239,618 amortization = $44,836,930.
